headline: Russia FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — Day 2 of post-format-offensive-surge. May 13 evidence: more than 100 Russian drones targeted Ukraine; at least 8 people killed in a barrage on civilian areas; Zelensky says Russia continues strikes deliberately targeting railway infrastructure and civilian sites in cities. Kremlin spokesman confirmed Russia resuming offensive after 'humanitarian ceasefire' expiration: 'the special military operation is continuing.' Russia accused Ukraine of 1,000+ ceasefire violations (counter-framing). The May 12 post-format-offensive-surge category, articulated in the May 12 floor record as the structural companion to May 11 strategic-format-exploitation, is now sustained through Day 2 (May 13). Floor sustained; conduct continuity documented.
rotation_state_data_integrity_flag: Rotation-state.json continues to show non-zero Russia composite (stale). Canonical countries.json value (0.0) used per hard constraint. Same staleness class flagged May 8-12.

conduct_documentation.summary: Day 2 of post-format-offensive-surge conduct continuity. May 13 evidence: 100+ drones targeting Ukraine; 8+ killed in civilian-area barrage; railway infrastructure and civilian sites in cities deliberately targeted. Kremlin confirms 'special military operation is continuing.' Floor sustained.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: Day 2 post-format-offensive-surge sustain (May 13): 100+ drones targeting Ukraine; 8+ killed in barrage on civilian areas. Zelensky framing: Russia continuing strikes 'deliberately targeting railway infrastructure and civilian sites in cities.'

Kremlin posture (May 13): Spokesman confirms 'special military operation is continuing' — formal continuation framing post-ceasefire expiration. Russia counter-frames Ukraine as ceasefire-violator (1,000+ alleged violations during the May 9-11 window).

Trump administration ceasefire diplomacy (May 13 carry-over): Trump talks of 'possible peace' as Kyiv emboldened by Russia's loss of territory in April (first net Russian territorial loss since 2024). Russian spring offensive 'floundered' per multiple analyses.
conduct_documentation.structural_continuity_with_may_11_12_fin: The May 11 floor record articulated strategic-format-exploitation (bad-faith conduct DURING humanitarian format). The May 12 floor record articulated post-format-offensive-surge (bad-faith conduct AFTER format expiry). The May 13 day-2 evidence sustains the post-format-offensive-surge category: 100+ drones the second night after format expiry; deliberate targeting of civilian-protected infrastructure (railway) and civilian sites in cities. The combined three-cycle pattern documents a complete bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle: PREPARE DURING (May 9-11) + STRIKE AFTER NIGHT 1 (May 11-12 overnight, 200+ drones) + SUSTAIN NIGHT 2 (May 12-13 day-2, 100+ drones). The conduct continuity confirms that the post-format-offensive-surge is a sustained operational tempo, not a single discrete event.
conduct_documentation.civilian_protected_site_targeting_contin: Railway infrastructure (May 13 Zelensky framing) is a category-level civilian-protected-target (transportation/civilian-mobility). Combined with prior cycle's strikes on residential buildings, a kindergarten in Kyiv region, and energy infrastructure in Mykolaiv, the pattern of civilian-protected-site targeting is sustained across all 3 days of post-format period.
conduct_documentation.diplomatic_posture_dissonance: Trump 'possible peace' framing concurrent with Russia's confirmed-continuing offensive operations creates structural diplomatic-posture-vs-operational-conduct dissonance. This is a continuation of the prior cycle's 'Putin signaled readiness to meet Zelensky but provided no format/date/location' performative-diplomacy finding.
conduct_documentation.russian_territorial_loss_context_signal: Independent reporting that Russian forces recorded net loss of territory last month (April) for first time since 2024 is a contextual signal — not Russia compassion behavior, but operational context. Does not affect floor.
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Six-event cumulative breach record (May 5-11) + strategic-format-exploitation (May 11) + post-format-offensive-surge (May 12) + post-format-offensive-surge Day 2 sustain (May 13) provides the densest single-entity floor-conduct documentation in the active rotation. Each successive day of post-format strike sustain compounds the floor-exit evidentiary burden.

key_evidence: May 13 2026 (in-window): More than 100 Russian drones targeted areas of Ukraine; at least 8 people killed in another barrage of civilian areas. Zelensky states Russia continues strikes deliberately targeting railway infrastructure and civilian sites in cities (WSLS/AP, RTE).
May 13 2026 (in-window): Kremlin spokesman confirms Russia resuming offensive after humanitarian ceasefire ended: 'the special military operation is continuing.' Trump talks 'possible peace' (WSLS).

May 12 2026 (carry-over, day 1 of surge): 200+ drones overnight May 11-12; 174 combat engagements; 225 controlled air bombs; 5,516 kamikaze drones cumulative; residential buildings + kindergarten + energy infrastructure struck.
Russia counter-framing: Ministry of Defence accused Ukraine of more than 1,000 ceasefire violations.

Russian spring offensive 'floundered': Russian forces recorded net loss of territory in April for first time since 2024; Ukrainian defensive lines holding; Ukrainian forces contesting tactical initiative.
Carry-over: STRUCTURAL FINDING — complete bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle (PREPARE + STRIKE AFTER NIGHT 1 + SUSTAIN NIGHT 2) documented across May 9-13.

evidence.source: WSLS / AP — Russia presses its barrages of Ukraine as Trump talks of possible peace
evidence.finding: 100+ drones May 13; 8+ killed in civilian-area barrage; railway and civilian-site targeting; Kyiv emboldened by Russian territorial loss; diplomatic-posture-vs-operational-conduct dissonance.

evidence.source: RTE — Kremlin says no 'specifics' on ending Ukraine war
evidence.finding: Kremlin spokesman confirms 'special military operation is continuing' — formal continuation framing.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Zelenskyy says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires
evidence.finding: Day 1 of surge: 200+ drones overnight May 11-12 (carry-over).

evidence.source: Defense News — Ukraine and Russia fight on despite US-mediated ceasefire
evidence.finding: Mutual ceasefire-violation accusations; both-sides framing; ceasefire collapse documentation.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. Day 2 of post-format-offensive-surge documented as sustained conduct continuity.

negativeSignals: Day 2 of post-format-offensive-surge (100+ drones, 8+ killed in civilian-area barrage May 13); railway and civilian-site deliberate targeting; Kremlin 'special military operation is continuing' framing; sustained civilian-protected-site targeting pattern across all 3 post-format days; complete bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle documented (PREPARE + STRIKE NIGHT 1 + SUSTAIN NIGHT 2).
positiveSignals: None internal to the state. External: Russian territorial loss in April for first time since 2024 documents operational-effectiveness decline but is not Russia compassion behavior.
sector_context: Countries floor-cluster. Russia distinguished this cycle by sustained post-format-offensive-surge across Day 2; densest cumulative floor-conduct documentation in active rotation. Cross-cluster: post-format-offensive-surge category remains relevant for any future floor-entity ceasefire context (collateral methodology relevance).
open_actions_resolved: Complete bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle pattern documented across May 9-13
open_actions_carried: Watch: Day 3 sustain or escalation of post-format-offensive-surge
Watch: any Putin-Zelensky meeting format/date/location announcement
Floor exit criteria require: sustained good-faith conduct in future ceasefire context + non-instrumentalized engagement with ceasefire structure + no post-format escalation — none in trajectory
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
